narration: When oceans get too warm, corals evict their partners — and turn ghostly white.
visual_description: Hook visual: When oceans get too warm, corals evict their partners — and turn ghostly white.
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: When oceans get too warm, corals evict their partners — and turn ghostly white.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: When oceans get too warm, corals evict their partners — and turn ghostly white.

narration: Corals rely on tiny algae called zooxanthellae, which live inside them. This relationship is crucial for their survival.
visual_description: Context visual: Corals rely on tiny algae called zooxanthellae, which live inside them. This relationship is crucial for their survival.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: Corals rely on tiny algae called zooxanthellae, which live inside them. This relationship is crucial for their survival.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: Corals rely on tiny algae called zooxanthellae, which live inside them. This relationship is crucial for their survival.

narration: But when temperatures rise, corals stress out and kick out these vital partners, leading to bleaching.
visual_description: Escalation visual: But when temperatures rise, corals stress out and kick out these vital partners, leading to bleaching.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: But when temperatures rise, corals stress out and kick out these vital partners, leading to bleaching.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: But when temperatures rise, corals stress out and kick out these vital partners, leading to bleaching.

narration: According to NOAA, while some corals can recover, repeated heat stress increases mortality.
visual_description: Evidence visual: According to NOAA, while some corals can recover, repeated heat stress increases mortality.

ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: According to NOAA, while some corals can recover, repeated heat stress increases mortality.

narration: The fate of coral reefs rests on our actions. Protecting our oceans is essential for their survival.
